Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix load balancer: remove worker.conf conflict, rebalance workers

Root cause: nginx/conf.d/worker.conf was loaded on Server 1 alongside
api.conf, creating a competing server block on port 80 that routed ALL
traffic to the local API only — bypassing the load balancer entirely.
Server 2 was idle while Server 1 was at 100% CPU (load avg 674).

Fixes:
- Move worker.conf out of conf.d/ so Server 1 only loads api.conf
- Reduce Server 1 from 35→15 API workers (it also runs PG+Redis+nginx)
  15×2 OMP = 30 cores for API, leaving ~50 for PG/Redis/nginx/OS
- Weight upstream: Server 1 weight=1 (15 workers), Server 2 weight=2
  (35 workers) — sends ~67% of traffic to the dedicated API server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Per-recipient wrapped keys (v2), git merge driver, and auto-resolve

- Change file format to v2: each recipient gets their own wrapped data key
  instead of a single age blob for all recipients. This makes recipient
  additions merge-conflict-free since each is an independent TOML line.
- Add `shh merge` command as a git merge driver for semantic 3-way merge
  of .env.enc files (decrypts, merges, re-encrypts, recomputes MAC).
- Auto-resolve merge conflicts on load: when any shh command encounters a
  conflicted .env.enc, it pulls the 3 versions from git's index stages,
  performs 3-way merge, saves the result, and stages it automatically.
- Maintain backward compatibility: v1 files can still be read and decrypted;
  any write operation produces v2 format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate test suite from Node.js built-in test runner to Vitest (#9)

Node's built-in test runner hangs when combining CJS require() of ESM
modules with DOM shims (linkedom/happy-dom). Vitest handles ESM natively
and provides built-in happy-dom environment support, unblocking DOM
testing for the upcoming view layer extraction.

Changes:
- Convert all test files from CJS/node:assert to ESM/vitest
- Add vitest and happy-dom as dev dependencies
- Add vitest.config.js with happy-dom environment
- Update Makefile test targets to use vitest
- Update eslint config for ESM test files
- Remove custom test/test.js wrapper (vitest handles test organization)

Co-authored-by: Justin Guluarte <claude@jguluarte.com>

fix(infra): railway.toml — web service must use apps/web/Dockerfile not api

Root railway.toml was pointing to apps/api/Dockerfile which caused the web
service to build and run the NestJS API instead of Next.js app, crashing
on startup due to missing DATABASE_URL / JWT secrets.

Fixed: dockerfilePath = apps/web/Dockerfile + correct healthcheck path.
API service uses apps/api/railway.toml (root directory set to apps/api).

zero API calls. Tested: correct top-hit for Supabase, Clerk, and UI queries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add shared_subspace regularization, checkpoint resumption, eval robustness, and plots

Training:
- New shared_subspace regularization: per-layer, penalizes all directions except
  the bisector of normalized pi_A and pi_B LoRA updates; set as default
- Split batch config: batch_size=32/ga=2 for non-reg, batch_size=16/ga=4 for pi_reg
  (both effective batch=64; pi_reg constrained by 3 models in memory on A100 80GB)
- Periodic Trainer checkpoints (save_steps=100, keep last 2); auto-resume from
  last checkpoint if training is interrupted
- train.py: auto-detect available checkpoints, skip already-trained models,
  --train flag to force retrain specific models, --ref_dir for separate reference dir
- dataloader_num_workers=4, epochs=3

Evaluation:
- Auto-discover available checkpoints; always emit all 4 model keys (null if missing)
- Partial-result resumption: re-run fills only null entries (--from_scratch to override)
- Metadata (subliminal_type, base_model, checkpoint_dir, timestamp) stored in JSON
- Save after every model so crashes don't lose completed work
- Verbose logging: [FOUND]/[MISSING]/[SKIP]/[SAVED] at every decision point

Notebooks:
- notebooks/eval_plots.ipynb: bar charts for instruction following, subliminal probes
  (per probe type), and coding security rate; skips any metric with no data;
  supports overlaying multiple result files for cross-run comparison

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 4 时序感知 + 3个P0审计修复 (#133)

* feat: 记忆管线 Phase 4 — 时序感知（valid_from / valid_until）

memories 表增加 valid_from（事实生效时间）和 valid_until（事实失效时间），
让记忆有事实有效期，过期记忆默认不返回。

- DB Migration（幂等）：ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + 回填 valid_from = created_at
- MemoryEntry 模型：新增 valid_from / valid_until 可选字段
- MemoryStoreDB.add()：支持 valid_from 参数（默认 created_at）
- MemoryStoreDB.update()：支持 valid_until 参数
- _execute_update()：UPDATE 操作自动设 valid_until = now
- 所有 query 路径（query/query_shared/query_patterns/query_team/

commit.message:
perf(http1): optimize CRLF scan in decode hot path with memmem

Replace the memchr_iter(b'\n') + scalar validation loop in find_crlf()
with a direct memmem::find(buf, b"\r\n") call. The memmem searcher uses
SIMD-accelerated two-byte pattern matching, avoiding per-candidate
branch overhead for bare \n rejection.

asupersync-34ne4

commit.message:
fix: handle InfIntVal in extern call arguments (#273)

## Summary

Fixes two CI failures at head:

- **`sai-middleblock_test` (300/500 failures)**: `ClassCastException:
InfIntVal cannot be cast to BitVal` — the IR can emit untyped integer
literals for extern call arguments (clone session IDs, field list
indices, register indices, varbit lengths), but `execExternCall` assumed
they'd always be `BitVal`. Replaced all 8 fragile `as BitVal` casts with
the existing `intValue()` helper.

- **`lint` (clang-tidy)**: Transient GitHub 502s when downloading Boost
archives. Disabled the repository cache eviction step that was forcing
re-downloads on every run.

## Test plan

- [x] All 41 non-heavy tests pass locally
- [x] `sai-middleblock_test` passes (was 300/500 failures)
- [ ] CI green

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
feat: simplify audio source UI + add no-audio detection

Replace the 3-button mode selector (Mic / System / Both) with a
simple "Include system audio" checkbox toggle, defaulting to mic-only.
Matches Loom's web-app pattern: mic starts instantly with no picker;
toggle ON triggers getDisplayMedia() (both mode). Setting persists in
plugin settings so returning users don't reconfigure.

System-only mode removed — it was niche and confusing.

Also add silence detection in the canvas waveform rAF loop: after
10 s of recording, if the max frequency bin stays below threshold (5/255),
a contextual warning appears below the waveform. Message varies by mode:
mic-only vs both (system audio). Warning auto-hides when audio resumes.

When the user switches to Chinese (zh) locale, the HeadlineTicker
now fetches Chinese translations for all 5 headline articles in
parallel using the existing /articles/{id}/translate endpoint.

- As each translation resolves, the title updates to Chinese
- If a translation fails, the English title is silently kept
- titleMap resets on board / locale change to avoid stale data
